prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `ce8d95cc`.
codex_response:
Implemented `ce8d95cc` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule is: collapse each consecutive run of identical rows to one row, then do the same for columns. I used the summary hint as a starting hypothesis and kept it because it matched every official example; no `notes.md` was needed. No new shared primitive was added.

The verifier is in [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_ce8d95cc/verifier.py), and the generator is in [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_ce8d95cc/generator.py). The generator builds the minimal woven stripe pattern first, then expands only the background gaps between/around stripe rows and columns, which keeps the generated family visually aligned with the official task. Package wiring is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_ce8d95cc/__init__.py).
